content:
# John Chapter 19 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_019_page_001.jpg, JHN_019_page_002.jpg

1. Then therefore Pilatos took the Yeshua and scourged [him].

2. And the soldiers having plaited a crown of thorns placed [it] on the head of him and threw around him a purple garment,

3. and they were coming to him and saying, "Hail, the king of the Ioudaioi!" And they were giving him slaps.

4. And Pilatos again went outside and says to them, "Behold, I bring him out to you, that you may know that I find no guilt in him."

5. Then the Yeshua came outside, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. And he says to them, "Behold, the man!"

6. When therefore the chief priests and the attendants saw him, they cried out, saying, "Crucify, crucify!" Pilatos says to them, "Take him yourselves and crucify [him], for I find no guilt in him."

7. The Ioudaioi answered him, "We have a law, and according to the law he ought to die, because he made himself Son of God."

8. When therefore Pilatos heard this word, he was more afraid,

9. and entered into the Praetorium again and says to the Yeshua, "Where are you from?" But the Yeshua gave him no answer.

10. Then Pilatos says to him, "To me you do not speak? Do you not know that I have authority to release you and I have authority to crucify you?"

11. The Yeshua answered, "You would have no authority against me if it had not been given to you from above. Because of this, the [one] having handed me over to you has greater sin."

12. From this Pilatos was seeking to release him; but the Ioudaioi cried out, saying, "If you release this [one], you are not a friend of Kaisar. Everyone making himself a king speaks against Kaisar."

13. Then Pilatos, having heard these words, brought the Yeshua outside and sat on [the] judgment seat in a place called Stone Pavement, but in Hebrew, Gabbatha.

14. And it was [the] Preparation of the Passover; it was about [the] sixth hour. And he says to the Ioudaioi, "Behold, the king of you!"

15. Then those [ones] cried out, "Away, away! Crucify him!" Pilatos says to them, "The king of you shall I crucify?" The chief priests answered, "We have no king except Kaisar."

16. Then therefore he handed him over to them that he might be crucified. Then they took the Yeshua,

17. and carrying the cross for himself, he went out to the [place] called Place of [the] Skull, which is called in Hebrew, Golgotha,

18. where they crucified him, and with him two others, on this side and on that side, and the Yeshua in [the] middle.

19. And Pilatos also wrote a title and placed [it] on the cross. And it was written, "Yeshua the Nazoraios, the King of the Ioudaioi."

20. This title therefore many of the Ioudaioi read, because the place was near the city where the Yeshua was crucified; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, in Greek.

21. Then the chief priests of the Ioudaioi said to Pilatos, "Do not write, 'The King of the Ioudaioi,' but that that [one] said, 'I am King of the Ioudaioi.'"

22. Pilatos answered, "What I have written, I have written."

23. Then the soldiers, when they crucified the Yeshua, took the garments of him and made four parts, to each soldier a part, and the tunic. And the tunic was seamless, from the top woven through [the] whole.

24. Then they said to one another, "Let us not tear it but cast lots for it, whose it will be," that the scripture might be fulfilled, "They divided the garments of me among themselves, and upon the clothing of me they cast [a] lot." The soldiers therefore did these things.

25. And there stood by the cross of the Yeshua the mother of him and the sister of the mother of him, Maria the [wife] of Klopas, and Maria the Magdalene.

26. Then the Yeshua, having seen the mother and the disciple standing by whom he loved, says to the mother, "Woman, behold, the son of you."

27. Then he says to the disciple, "Behold, the mother of you." And from that hour the disciple took her into the own.

28. After this the Yeshua, knowing that already all things have been completed, that the scripture might be fulfilled, says, "I thirst."

29. A vessel was set there full of vinegar; then having put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, they brought [it] to the mouth of him.

30. When therefore the Yeshua received the vinegar, he said, "It is finished." And having bowed the head, he handed over the spirit.

31. Then the Ioudaioi, since it was [the] Preparation, that the bodies might not remain on the cross on the Sabbath — for that Sabbath was a great [day] — asked Pilatos that the legs of them might be broken and they might be taken away.

32. Then the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first and of the other [one] having been crucified with him;

33. but having come to the Yeshua, when they saw [that] he had already died, they did not break the legs of him,

34. but one of the soldiers pierced the side of him with a spear, and immediately there came out blood and water.

35. And the [one] having seen has testified, and true is the testimony of him, and that [one] knows that he speaks true things, that you also may believe.

36. For these things happened that the scripture might be fulfilled, "A bone of him will not be broken."

37. And again another scripture says, "They will look on the [one] whom they pierced."

38. And after these things Ioseph from Arimathaia — being a disciple of the Yeshua, but having been hidden because of the fear of the Ioudaioi — asked Pilatos that he might take the body of the Yeshua. And Pilatos permitted. Then he came and took the body of him.

39. And Nikodemos also came — the [one] having come to him at night at the first — bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred pounds.

40. Then they took the body of the Yeshua and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, just as it is [the] custom for the Ioudaioi to prepare for burial.

41. And in the place where he was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had ever been placed.

42. There therefore, because of the Preparation of the Ioudaioi, because the tomb was near, they placed the Yeshua.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.5 — "Behold, the man!" (Ἰδοὺ ὁ ἄνθρωπος):** Pilate's words — "Ecce homo" — are deeply ironic. He means: "Look at this pitiful figure — surely no threat." John means: behold the true human, the Son of Man, the one in whom God's glory is paradoxically revealed in suffering.

2. **v.14 — "The sixth hour":** About noon. This contradicts Mark 15:25 ("the third hour" = 9 AM). John's timing is likely theological: at noon on the Day of Preparation, the Passover lambs are being slaughtered in the temple. Jesus, the Lamb of God, is sentenced at the same hour.

3. **v.23 — The seamless tunic:** χιτὼν ἄραφος, ἐκ τῶν ἄνωθεν ὑφαντὸς δι' ὅλου — "without seam, woven from the top through the whole." This resembles the high priest's garment (Exodus 28:32). Jesus dies as both sacrifice and priest. The soldiers' refusal to tear it fulfills Psalm 22:18.

4. **v.29 — "Hyssop":** ὑσσώπῳ — hyssop was used to apply the blood of the Passover lamb to the doorposts (Exodus 12:22). John's use of this detail links Jesus' death to the original Passover.

5. **v.30 — "It is finished" (Τετέλεσται):** Perfect tense — "It has been completed and remains completed." Not a cry of defeat but of accomplishment. The work the Father gave (17:4) is done. The word can also mean "paid in full" — a term used on receipts.

6. **v.34 — "Blood and water":** The medical and theological significance are both debated. Medically, it may indicate pericardial effusion. Theologically, early interpreters saw baptism (water) and eucharist (blood). John emphasizes eyewitness testimony (v.35) — this really happened.

— EMET, AI Translator
